_note: Researched dated figures for build_metro_cards.py. Every value verified against a live page during the 2026-06-12 gate-3 session; source URL + as_of recorded per field. annual_m is millions of rides/yr; route_km is km; fare_usd is the minimum adult single ride converted to USD on fx_as_of; farebox is recovery ratio percent (vintages and scopes vary by city, see notes; null where no figure is published). NO em dashes anywhere.

opened.what: Ginza Line, Asakusa to Ueno, 30 December 1927: the first subway in Asia.

route_km.note: Tokyo Metro 195 km plus Toei 109 km, per the Japan Subway Association.

ridership.note: Sum of the two operators' daily averages annualized (Metro 6.516M/day plus Toei 2.488M/day); the operators publish daily, not annual, totals.

headway.note: Marunouchi Line, about 1 min 50 s at peak.

driverless.note: All Metro and Toei lines run with drivers (GoA2 at most).

fare_usd.note: Cheapest single, shortest distance band on either operator.

farebox.note: Tokyo Metro component (Toei FY2018 was 171.46); fares far exceed operating cost.

opened.what: Line 1 opened in 1974, the network's first line.

route_km.note: The full capital-region network as the familiar map draws it, matching the declared scope.

ridership.note: PLACEHOLDER: covers Seoul Metro lines 1-8 only; no single whole-network annual total is published. Korail lines added about 1.16 billion in 2017.

headway.note: Line 2, about 2 min 18 s at peak.

span_h.note: Trains run roughly 05:30 to midnight; varies by line.

driverless.note: Sinbundang, Ui-Sinseol, Sillim, Uijeongbu LRT, Yongin Everline, Gimpo Goldline, Incheon Line 2.

fare_usd.note: Base transport-card fare for trips up to 10 km, in effect since 28 June 2025.

farebox.note: Seoul city statement: fares covered about 60 percent of operating cost before the 2023 and 2025 fare hikes.

opened.what: First North South Line section, five stations, opened 7 November 1987.

route_km.note: MRT heavy metro only; LRT excluded.

ridership.note: LTA average daily MRT ridership 3.412M, annualized; LRT excluded.

headway.note: Every 2 to 3 minutes at peak; busiest lines at the low end.

driverless.note: North East, Circle, Downtown and Thomson-East Coast all fully automated.

fare_usd.note: Adult stored-value fare for journeys up to 3.2 km.

farebox.note: Operator SMRT, not the whole MRT network; no network-wide ratio published.

opened.what: The Modified Initial System, now part of the Kwun Tong Line, opened 1 October 1979; the absorbed East Rail corridor dates to 1910.

route_km.note: Heavy rail only; the 36 km Light Rail network excluded.

ridership.note: MTR 2025 patronage: Domestic 1,594.4M plus Cross-boundary 106.7M plus Airport Express 13.3M; Light Rail and bus excluded.

span_h.note: About 19 hours a day, roughly 05:30 to 01:00.

driverless.note: Disneyland Resort Line (since 2005) and South Island Line.

fare_usd.note: Cheapest one-stop adult fare, Octopus or single ticket.

farebox.note: Fare revenue over operating cost; excludes MTR's large property income.

opened.what: Line 1, Porte Maillot to Porte de Vincennes, opened 19 July 1900 during the World's Fair.

route_km.note: Metro lines only after the June 2024 extensions; RER excluded.

headway.note: Line 14, automated, signalled for an 85 s peak headway.

span_h.note: About 05:30 to 01:15 on weekdays, later on weekend nights.

fare_usd.note: Navigo Liberte+ pay-as-you-go single, the cheapest regular metro fare.

farebox.note: Ile-de-France Mobilites, all regional modes, not the Metro alone.

opened.what: First Line 1 section entered service on 28 May 1993; full Line 1 opened April 1995.

route_km.note: 19 lines (1 to 18 plus Pujiang); maglev excluded.

headway.note: Line 9, about 1 min 50 s at peak.

span_h.note: Most lines run roughly 05:30 to 22:30, later on weekend nights.

fare_usd.note: Distance-based: 3 yuan covers the first 6 km.

farebox.note: No systemwide farebox recovery figure published.

opened.what: First line opened 1 October 1969; ticketed public access from January 1971. The system dates itself from 1969.

route_km.note: The operator's full mapped network incl. both airport expresses, S1 and Xijiao.

headway.note: Line 4, about 1 min 43 s in the morning peak.

span_h.note: Most lines run roughly 05:00 to 23:00; varies by line.

driverless.note: Yanfang, Daxing Airport, Lines 11, 17, 3 and 12 run unattended (GoA4).

fare_usd.note: Distance-based: 3 yuan covers the first 6 km.

opened.what: The Metropolitan Railway opened 10 January 1863, the world's first underground passenger railway.

route_km.note: Underground only (11 lines); Elizabeth line, Overground and DLR excluded.

ridership.note: 1.181 billion journeys in TfL year 2023/24, Underground only.

headway.note: Victoria line, about 36 trains an hour, one every 100 s.

span_h.note: Weekday span about 05:00 to midnight; the Night Tube runs weekends only.

driverless.note: Automatic train operation on several lines, but every train has a driver.

fare_usd.note: Off-peak pay-as-you-go single, one zone outside Zone 1, from 1 March 2026.

opened.what: The first underground IRT line opened 27 October 1904; elevated rapid transit ran from 1868 but is not part of today's subway.

route_km.note: 248 route miles, 472 stations; Staten Island Railway and PATH excluded. Revenue track is far longer.

ridership.note: Nearly 1.3 billion subway trips in 2025, up 7 percent; subway only.

headway.note: Peak intervals 2 to 10 min; the CBTC 7 line runs about every 2 min.

span_h.note: Genuine 24/7 service every day of the year.

driverless.note: Highest automation is GoA2 CBTC on the L and 7, with operators aboard.

fare_usd.note: Base OMNY fare from January 2026; a 7-day cap limits spending to USD 35.

farebox.note: MTA NYC Transit, subway and bus combined, a pandemic year.

opened.what: Line 1, Cuatro Caminos to Sol, opened by King Alfonso XIII on 17 October 1919.

route_km.note: Metro de Madrid (lines 1 to 12 plus Ramal); the 28 km Metro Ligero excluded.

ridership.note: All-time record 715.2M trips in 2024, up 8 percent; heavy metro only.

headway.note: Main lines every 2 to 4 minutes in rush hours.

driverless.note: Automation tops out at GoA2 with drivers; Line 6 conversion is due 2027.

fare_usd.note: Zone A single, up to 5 stations.

farebox.note: Most recent Madrid figure in the comparative table; no newer ratio found.

opened.what: First line opened 15 May 1935, 11 km and 13 stations from Sokolniki to Okhotny Ryad.

route_km.note: 535.3 km incl. the 54 km MCC ring (line 14); the MCD diameters are excluded.

ridership.note: Metro 2,194M plus the MCC's 119M for 2025, matching the scope (metro plus MCC).

headway.note: About 90 s at rush hour; cut to 80 s in 2023, reported a world first.

span_h.note: First trains about 05:25; all entrances close at 01:00.

driverless.note: No metro or MCC line runs unattended passenger service.

fare_usd.note: One trip on metro or MCC with a Troika card, from January 2026.

farebox.note: No recent published farebox recovery ratio found.

opened.what: First stretch opened 19 October 2002, fully driverless from day one.

route_km.note: Metro M1 to M4 only, 44 stations, incl. the June 2024 M4 Sydhavn extension; S-train excluded.

headway.note: Every 2 min on the M1/M2 shared core at peak.

span_h.note: Round-the-clock every day, at reduced night frequency.

driverless.note: All four lines fully automated and unattended since each opened.

fare_usd.note: Lowest adult single (2 zones), covering metro, bus and train in the zones.

farebox.note: Among the highest in the comparative table; profits help repay construction debt.

opened.what: Red Line, Shahdara to Tis Hazari, opened 24 December 2002, public service from 25 December.

route_km.note: DMRC's 10 lines incl. the Airport Express, after the 8 March 2026 Phase 4 openings; Noida, Gurugram and RRTS excluded.

ridership.note: 235.8 crore corridor-wise journeys in 2025; daily average 6.46M.

span_h.note: Airport Express from 04:45, regular lines 05:00 to about 23:35.

driverless.note: Magenta (India's first, 2020) and Pink run unattended, about 120 km.

fare_usd.note: 0 to 2 km slab, first revision since 2017 (effective 25 August 2025); smart cards get 10 percent off.

farebox.note: Fare-only share not disclosed; FY25 operating revenue beat operating cost by about 110 percent incl. non-fare income.

opened.what: Line 1, Xilang to Huangsha, opened 28 June 1997, the fourth metro in mainland China.

route_km.note: 19-line network incl. the Guangfo line and the APM; Foshan's own lines, trams and intercity rail excluded.

ridership.note: Operator annual report: 3.399 billion rides in 2025; daily average 9.31M.

headway.note: Line 3 trunk, about 1 min 58 s at peak.

span_h.note: About 18 hours daily, roughly 06:00 to midnight.

driverless.note: APM plus Lines 10, 11 and 12 run fully automatic (GoA4).

fare_usd.note: Distance-based from 2 yuan; the APM is a flat 2 yuan.

farebox.note: No fare-revenue-to-cost split published; group finances lean on property.

opened.what: Line 1, Insurgentes to Zaragoza, opened 4 September 1969.

route_km.note: Route length in revenue service (STC headlines 226.5 km with maintenance track); Tren Ligero and Suburbano excluded.

ridership.note: 1,241.6M trips in 2025, up 6 percent, lifted by Line 1's full reopening.

headway.note: Line 1 after its rebuild, the city's announced plan.

span_h.note: All lines 05:00 to midnight on weekdays.

fare_usd.note: Flat fare held at 5 pesos for 2026; paid by rechargeable card.

farebox.note: No official ratio; the city says each trip costs it about 13 pesos against the 5-peso fare.

opened.what: Line 1, Helwan to Ramses Square, opened 27 September 1987 as Africa's first metro; it absorbed the older Helwan and El Marg suburban railways.

route_km.note: Lines 1 to 3 only, after Line 3's final phases; LRT and monorail excluded.

ridership.note: Approximate; about 5M riders a day. The operator does not publish a clean annual series.

headway.note: Line 2's tightest documented interval (2.7 to 3 min).

farebox.note: No published ratio; the March 2026 fare rise cited rising fuel and maintenance cost.
